S.text: Glaucoma follow-up for POAG OU on maximal medical therapy: latanoprost qHS, timolol BID, brimonidine BID, all OU.
Imperfect adherence due to complex regimen, especially the midday brimonidine dose.
Reports increased effort with near/reading vision OD.

O.text: IOP 26 mmHg OD / 24 mmHg OS (GAT); 21/20 four months ago; baseline 34/32; above target.
Optic nerves: C/D 0.85 OD, progressed from prior 0.8 with thinner inferior rim; OS 0.8 stable; no fresh disc hemorrhage.
HVF 24-2: superior defect OD deepened and expanded vs 4 months ago with worse mean deviation; OS stable.
OCT RNFL: progressive inferior RNFL thinning OD.

A.text: Primary open-angle glaucoma OU; OD progressing (structural and functional) on maximal medical therapy with IOP above target; OS stable.

P.text: Refer to glaucoma surgeon for surgical options (trabeculectomy or drainage device) for OD; target appointment within 2 weeks.
Continue latanoprost, timolol, and brimonidine unchanged until surgery.
